selection_reason: Top 3 positive drifters vs frozen reference.json. opt/user_priority.md does not exist (harness R34 removed it). Selection is drift-evidence only.

candidates.drift_notes: latest.json shows +50.8%, single-shot live run shows +35% - both well above 5% fail threshold; regression is real, magnitude varies with measurement jitter.

observations.claim: Inside create_and_sum loop body B1, GetGlobal appears 2x per iteration (v10, v19 at /tmp/cg_oc_create_and_sum.log:17,22) and is NOT hoisted to preheader B4, despite both globals (globals[0]=new_vec3, globals[5]=vec3_add) being loop-invariant.
observations.evidence: Direct IR read. B1 preds=[B2] (loop body), B4 preds=[B0] (preheader); v10 and v19 live in B1 instead of B4.
observations.impact: 2 redundant global lookups x 200000 iterations = 400K wasted loads; each GetGlobal is a GoFunction call boundary triggering caller-saved spill/reload.

observations.claim: Inside transform_chain loop body B1, GetGlobal appears 3x per iteration (v10, v15, v18 at /tmp/cg_oc_transform_chain.log:17,19,21) - none hoisted.
observations.evidence: Direct IR read. 3 GetGlobal (globals[0]=new_vec3, globals[6]=vec3_add, globals[7]=vec3_scale) all live in B1, preheader B4 only holds ConstFloat hoists.
observations.impact: 3 x 500000 = 1.5M redundant global lookups. Combined with the 2 Call ops per iteration, this loop is call-overhead bound.

observations.claim: create_and_sum and transform_chain have 0 GetField/SetField/NewTable in their IR - all allocation work happens inside the callees (new_vec3). The outer functions are CALL-bound, not allocation-bound.
observations.evidence: Direct IR parse - regex count over printed IR shows 0 occurrences of GetField/SetField/NewTable in the two outer functions; new_vec3 (the leaf) has 1 NewTable + 3 SetField.
observations.impact: Optimizing new_vec3 call overhead (inlining, or fast-path NEWTABLE+3xSETFIELD) directly attacks the dominant cost. LICM alone will not help because GetGlobal is inside the body.

observations.claim: Memory operations are 57-62% of instructions for all three object_creation functions; integer/float compute is only 30-32%.
observations.evidence: Capstone disasm classification at /tmp/cg_disasm_summary.json: create_and_sum 466/813 memory, transform_chain 573/988, new_vec3 129/208.
observations.impact: Memory-bound even though NumSpills=0 - the loads/stores are all NaN-box/unbox, GetGlobal slot loads, and caller-saved save/restore around BL. Suggests register pressure is not the issue; call-convention is.

observations.claim: SetField lowering in new_vec3 emits ~60 memory ops per 3-field SetField - 129 memory ops for a function with only NewTable + 3xSetField.
observations.evidence: Disasm: 129 memory insns / 3 SetField = ~43 memory ops per field write in isolation; some are frame setup, but SetField appears to lower to roughly 10-15 loads/stores each, implying shape-check + bucket write + write barrier. Not hand-traced; dominant mnemonics at /tmp/cg_disasm_summary.json to confirm.
observations.impact: If SetField lowering is heavier than a Lua-like 2-insn store-to-slot + shape-stable update, that is a direct target for object_creation.

observations.claim: The regression (reference 0.764s -> 1.152s, +50.8%) appeared AFTER reference was frozen at a388f782 on 2026-04-11T14:55:55Z. Candidate bisect commits live in the set {598bc1e, 39b5ef3, 4b321fb, 144c1a4, c375913, 56b19e7}.
observations.evidence: git log a388f78..HEAD -- internal/methodjit internal/vm internal/compiler (see bisect_candidates). 39b5ef3 touches Shape system (direct suspect for SetField cost); 598bc1e self-call check could penalize recursive vec3_add inline path.
observations.impact: A bisect across these 6 commits would identify the specific regression source instead of speculating at the fix.

candidates.drift_notes: latest.json shows +16.7% but single-shot live is only +7.1%. This is borderline - the benchmark is fast (tens of ms) so per-run jitter is large. Still above 5% fail threshold in latest.json.

observations.claim: make_random_array FAILS Tier 2 compilation with 'methodjit: finalize error: jit: unresolved label arith_done_11'. This is a compile-pipeline bug, not a fallback-by-design.
observations.evidence: Direct test output at /tmp/cg_sort_make_random_array.log:3. make_random_array is called 3 times per REP to build a 50000-element array - the full array-generation cost falls to Tier 1.
observations.impact: Tier 2 compile failure means the array-build phase of sort runs at Tier 1 speed. This is a LATENT BUG independent of any drift - needs a separate diagnostic regardless of sort regression.

observations.claim: quicksort code size is 6408 bytes for an 8-block IR with 16 SSA values - ~400 bytes per SSA value, ~800 bytes per block.
observations.evidence: Log line 'code: size=6408 bytes, DirectEntryOffset=5528, NumSpills=1'. Subtracting the 5528-byte prologue/trampoline leaves ~880 bytes of body code for ~29 IR instructions = ~30 insns per IR op (1602 disasm / ~50 non-trivial IR insns).
observations.impact: Tier 2 output is bulky. GetTable/SetTable each lower to a large sequence including shape check + type dispatch. This is structural, not a drift symptom.

observations.claim: quicksort inner partition loop uses GetTable/SetTable 6+4 times per iteration with Type=any - no GuardType narrows arr[j] before comparison.
observations.evidence: IR at /tmp/cg_sort_quicksort.log B3-B4 shows v17=GetTable, v19=Le (not LeInt), v23/v25=GetTable, v26/v27=SetTable. LeInt would require the array elements to be narrowed; they stay 'any'.
observations.impact: Every element comparison is polymorphic. Typed array (kinded table) or element-feedback-based narrowing would collapse 'any'-path into int-path.

observations.claim: quicksort recursive tail: two 'v46 = GetGlobal globals[0]; v50 = Call v46, ...' pairs in B6 - each recursive call resolves quicksort through a global lookup.
observations.evidence: /tmp/cg_sort_quicksort.log B6 block; v46 and v51 are GetGlobal globals[0] repeated.
observations.impact: Self-recursive call should use a direct BL to the function's own entry, not GetGlobal + indirect Call. 144c1a4 (R28 'self-call fast path') may already handle this but clearly not via GetGlobal elimination.

candidates.drift_notes: latest.json +11% but single-shot live run is 0.026s - BELOW reference. closure_bench total is 26ms - noise floor is a significant fraction. Include as candidate but ANALYZE should treat as lower priority than object_creation.

observations.claim: In test_accumulator loop body B1, 'v13 = Add v22, v11 : any' uses the polymorphic 'Add' op, NOT 'AddInt'. v22 is an int phi (initialized to 0 = ConstInt), but v11 is the return value of the closure counter() and is typed 'any', poisoning the sum.
observations.evidence: /tmp/cg_closure_accumulator.log:18 shows 'v13 = Add v22, v11 : any'. v22 is Phi B0:v2, B1:v13 : int but is typed int because of v2=ConstInt 0.
observations.impact: Polymorphic Add runs through the slow-path type-dispatch block every iteration. 1,000,000 iters x slow-Add vs. fast-AddInt is the loop's entire budget. If the closure's return type were narrowed (feedback or return-type inference), this would collapse to AddInt.

observations.claim: Similarly in test_closure_call B1, 'v14 = Add v22, v12 : any' is polymorphic. v12 is the return of add5(i) which is a closure call. Same 'any'-typed return poisoning.
observations.impact: Same as above - 100000 iters affected. R12 'feedback-typed loads' was abandoned because FeedbackVector was empty at Tier 2 compile time; the return-type narrowing path never landed.

observations.claim: Live run measured 0.026s - actually BELOW reference 0.027s. latest.json's 0.030s appears to be a noise spike, not a real regression.
observations.impact: ANALYZE should NOT invest round budget chasing closure_bench as a regression. The structural observations above (polymorphic Add) are still load-bearing and relevant to any closure-focused initiative, but not for this drift signal.

bisect_candidates.subject: R32 Task 1 - LoopScalarPromotionPass for nbody float loop-carried pairs
R31 Task 1 - SimplifyPhisPass (Braun Algorithm 5)
bisect_candidates.suspect_for: phi simplification could affect hoist candidates

bisect_candidates.subject: Revert R30 Task 1 (GETGLOBAL transient classification)
bisect_candidates.suspect_for: HIGH - direct revert of a GETGLOBAL change; object_creation drift is GetGlobal-in-loop

bisect_candidates.subject: R30 Task 1 - restore fib via transient OP_GETGLOBAL classification
bisect_candidates.suspect_for: HIGH - the same change that was reverted

bisect_candidates.subject: R28 Task 1 - ctx.Regs lazy flush on self-call fast path
Shape system, GC scan all regs, split table.go, fix empty-loop test
bisect_candidates.suspect_for: HIGH - Shape system change directly affects SetField/NewTable lowering; object_creation is allocation-heavy

bisect_candidates.subject: self-call DirectEntryPtr check prevents goroutine stack overflow

cross_cutting_observations.claim: GetGlobal-in-loop is a shared pattern across object_creation and sort - both benchmarks have loop-invariant GetGlobal that LICM does not hoist.
cross_cutting_observations.evidence: create_and_sum B1 has 2 GetGlobal; transform_chain B1 has 3 GetGlobal; quicksort B6 has 2 GetGlobal; all are inside reachable loop/recursion paths.
cross_cutting_observations.impact: A single LICM enhancement (hoist GetGlobal across body if body has no SetGlobal to same key) would attack both object_creation and sort. The R30 revert (4455fcf) may have removed exactly this kind of reasoning.

cross_cutting_observations.claim: None of the three candidate benchmarks have GuardType on their call-result SSA values; polymorphic Add/Le persist across loop iterations.
cross_cutting_observations.evidence: IR parse: 0 GuardType in object_creation functions; 2 in quicksort (param only, not call-result); 0 in closure_bench functions.
cross_cutting_observations.impact: Return-type inference (or feedback-typed calls) would unlock AddInt/LeInt fast paths. R12 noted FeedbackVector empty at Tier 2; a static return-type propagator would work even without runtime feedback.

bugs_discovered.description: make_random_array in sort.gs cannot Tier 2 compile: 'methodjit: finalize error: jit: unresolved label arith_done_11'. The function uses overflow-checked integer arithmetic (LCG with mul+add+mod on 32-bit range) - the arith_done_N label is the overflow fallthrough target and is not being patched.
bugs_discovered.impact: Benchmark silently falls back to Tier 1 for array generation - this alone could account for part of sort regression.

validation.authoritative_pipeline: profileTier2Func -> BuildGraph -> RunTier2Pipeline -> AllocateRegisters -> Compile. Matches production Method JIT compile path. No hand-constructed IR, no synthetic TypeFloat/TypeInt.
validation.benchmark_source: benchmarks/suite/*.gs - real benchmark sources used by the reference/latest measurements.
validation.reference_immutable: benchmarks/data/reference.json NOT modified. frozen_from_commit a388f782 preserved.
validation.temp_tests_cleanup: internal/methodjit/context_gather_*_test.go files will be deleted at end of phase.
